title: The Squatterï؟½s Man
content:
آ آ آ آ Come, all ye lads anï؟½ list to me,
آ آ آ آ Thatï؟½s left your homes anï؟½ crossed the sea,
آ آ آ آ To try your fortune, bound or free,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ All in this golden land.
آ آ آ آ For twelve long months I had to pace,
آ آ آ آ Humping my swag with a cadging face,
آ آ آ آ Sleeping in the bush, like the sable race,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ As in my song youï؟½ll understand.

آ آ آ آ Unto this country I did come,
آ آ آ آ A regular out-and-out new chum.
آ آ آ آ I then abhorred the sight of rumï؟½
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Teetotal was my plan.
آ آ آ آ But soon I learned to wet one eyeï؟½
آ آ آ آ Misfortune oft-times made me sigh.
آ آ آ آ To raise fresh funds I was forced to fly,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And be a squatterï؟½s man.

آ آ آ آ Soon at a station I appeared.
آ آ آ آ I saw the squatter with his beard,
آ آ آ آ And up to him I boldly steered,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ With my swag and billy-can.

آ آ آ آ I said, ï؟½Kind sir, I want a job!ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Said he, ï؟½Do you know how to snob
آ آ آ آ Or can you break in a bucking cob?ï؟½
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Whilst my figure he well did scan.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½ï؟½Tis now I want a useful cove
آ آ آ آ To stop at home and not to rove.
آ آ آ آ The scamps go aboutï؟½a regular droveï؟½
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ I ï؟½spose youï؟½re one of the clan?
آ آ آ آ But Iï؟½ll give tenï؟½ten, sugar anï؟½ tea;
آ آ آ آ Ten bob a week, if youï؟½ll suit me,
آ آ آ آ And very soon I hope youï؟½ll be
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ A handy squatterï؟½s man.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½At daylight you must milk the cows,
آ آ آ آ Make butter, cheese, anï؟½ feed the sows,
آ آ آ آ Put on the kettle, the cook arouse,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And clean the family shoes.
آ آ آ آ The stable anï؟½ sheep yard clean out,
آ آ آ آ And always answer when we shout,
آ آ آ آ With ï؟½Yes, maï؟½am,ï؟½ andآ آ آ آ ï؟½No, sir,ï؟½ mind your mouth;
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And my youngsters donï؟½t abuse.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½You must fetch wood anï؟½ water, bake anï؟½ boil,
آ آ آ آ Act as butcher when we kill;
آ آ آ آ The corn anï؟½ taters you must hill,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Keep the garden spick and span.

آ آ آ آ You must not scruple in the rain
آ آ آ آ To take to market all the grain.
آ آ آ آ Be sure you come sober back again
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ To be a squatterï؟½s man.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ He sent me to an old bark hut,
آ آ آ آ Inhabited by a greyhound slut,
آ آ آ آ Who put her fangs through my poor fut,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And, snarling, off she ran.
آ آ آ آ So once more Iï؟½m looking for a job,
آ آ آ آ Without a copper in my fob.
آ آ آ آ With Ben Hall or Gardiner Iï؟½d rather rob,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Than be a squatterï؟½s man.
meaning:
Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton)'s contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Squatterï؟½s Man"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
